description: KLX WordPress CLI — a dynamic, fault-tolerant CLI for the WordPress REST API built with Bun and TypeScript. Auto-discovers routes from /wp-json and generates commands at runtime.

userStories.title: Initialize Bun project scaffolding
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the project set up with Bun so that I can start building the CLI with proper TypeScript support and tooling.
acceptanceCriteria: `package.json` with project name `wpklx`, `bin` field pointing to `src/index.ts`, and scripts: `dev`, `build`, `typecheck`, `lint`
`tsconfig.json` configured for Bun with strict mode
`bunfig.toml` with relevant Bun configuration
`src/index.ts` entry point that prints version and exits
`bun run dev` executes the CLI in development mode
`bun run build` produces a production build
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define TypeScript types for config, CLI, and API
userStories.description: As a developer, I want strict TypeScript types for all data structures so that the codebase is type-safe from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/types/config.ts` exports: `EnvConfig`, `YamlProfile`, `YamlConfig`, `ResolvedConfig` types
`src/types/cli.ts` exports: `ParsedArgs`, `Command`, `GlobalFlags` types
`src/types/api.ts` exports: `Route`, `RouteParam`, `DiscoveredSchema`, `ApiResponse`, `ApiError` types
All types are exported and importable from their respective modules
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build logging helper with verbosity levels
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a logger that respects verbosity settings so that debug info only appears when requested.
acceptanceCriteria: Logger in `src/helpers/logger.ts` with methods: `debug()`, `info()`, `warn()`, `error()`
`debug()` only outputs when verbose mode is enabled
`info()` outputs normally, suppressed in quiet mode
`warn()` always outputs to stderr
`error()` always outputs to stderr
All output respects `--no-color` flag and `NO_COLOR` env var
Timestamps included in verbose mode
Logger accepts a config object or setter to configure verbose/quiet/no-color state
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Parse .env configuration files
userStories.description: As a user with a single WordPress site, I want to configure the CLI via a `.env` file so that I can get started quickly without YAML.
acceptanceCriteria: Reads `.env` from current working directory by default
Supports an optional custom `.env` file path parameter
Parses all supported env vars: `WP_HOST`, `WP_USERNAME`, `WP_APPLICATION_PASSWORD`, `WP_API_PREFIX`, `WP_PER_PAGE`, `WP_TIMEOUT`, `WP_VERIFY_SSL`, `WP_OUTPUT_FORMAT`
Returns a typed config object matching the `EnvConfig` type from `src/types/config.ts`
Throws a clear error (exit code 2) if required vars (`WP_HOST`, `WP_USERNAME`, `WP_APPLICATION_PASSWORD`) are missing when .env file is the only config source
Ignores unknown env vars without error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Parse YAML profile configuration
userStories.description: As a user managing multiple WordPress sites, I want to define profiles in a YAML file so that I can switch between sites with `@name` syntax.
acceptanceCriteria: Searches for config file in order: `./wpklx.config.yaml` then `~/.config/wpklx/config.yaml`
Parses `default` key to determine which profile to use when no `@name` is specified
Parses `profiles` map with keys as profile names, each containing: `host`, `username`, `application_password`, `api_prefix`, `per_page`, `timeout`, `verify_ssl`, `output_format`
Returns the active profile as a typed `YamlProfile` object
Throws a clear error (exit code 2) if the requested profile does not exist
Throws a clear error (exit code 2) if required fields (`host`, `username`, `application_password`) are missing from the active profile
Uses the `yaml` npm package for YAML parsing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Resolve @profile from CLI arguments
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type `@staging` anywhere in my command to switch profiles so that multi-site workflows are seamless.
acceptanceCriteria: Detects `@name` token anywhere in `process.argv`
Extracts the profile name and removes the token from the argument list before further parsing
If no `@name` is present, uses the `default` profile from YAML config (or falls back to `.env`)
If `@name` is present but no YAML config exists, throws error (exit code 2) with message suggesting to create `wpklx.config.yaml`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Merge configuration from all sources
userStories.description: As a user, I want CLI flags to override `.env` values, which override YAML profile values, which override defaults, so that configuration is predictable.
acceptanceCriteria: Implements merge order: CLI flags > `.env` file > active YAML profile > built-in defaults
Built-in defaults: `api_prefix: "/wp-json"`, `per_page: 20`, `timeout: 30000`, `verify_ssl: true`, `output_format: "table"`
CLI flags `--format`, `--per-page`, `--page`, `--env` override all other sources
Returns a single fully-resolved `ResolvedConfig` object with all required fields guaranteed present
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Parse CLI arguments into structured command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type `wpklx post list --status draft` and have it parsed into a structured command so that the CLI can route it correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Parses `<resource>` as the first positional argument (after removing `@profile` and global flags)
Parses `<action>` as the second positional argument
Resolves action shortcuts: `ls`->`list`, `show`->`get`, `new`->`create`, `edit`->`update`, `rm`->`delete`
Parses `--key value` pairs as command options
Parses `--flag` (no value) as boolean `true`
Detects positional ID: `wpklx post show 42` treats `42` as `--id 42`
Extracts global flags (`--format`, `--fields`, `--per-page`, `--page`, `--quiet`, `--verbose`, `--no-color`, `--help`, `--version`, `--env`) before resource-specific options
Returns a `ParsedArgs` object with `{ resource, action, id?, options, globalFlags }`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Format API errors into human-readable messages
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear error messages so that I know what went wrong and how to fix it.
acceptanceCriteria: Error helper in `src/helpers/error.ts` formats WordPress REST API error responses (code + message fields) into readable output
Auth errors (401/403): message includes 'Check your application password at WP Admin > Users > Profile > Application Passwords'
Not found (404): message includes 'Run `wpklx routes` to see available commands'
Validation errors (400/422): shows field-level validation messages from the API response
Network errors: 'Could not connect to {host}. Check the URL and your network connection'
Timeout errors: 'Request timed out after {timeout}ms. Increase timeout with WP_TIMEOUT or --timeout'
SSL errors: 'SSL certificate verification failed. Use WP_VERIFY_SSL=false for local development'
Each error type exits with the correct exit code (1=general, 2=config, 3=auth, 4=not found, 5=validation, 6=network/timeout)
Error output goes to stderr via the logger
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff
userStories.description: As a user, I want transient network failures to be retried automatically so that flaky connections don't block my workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Standalone helper in `src/helpers/retry.ts` — generic async retry function, not HTTP-specific
Retries on network errors and HTTP 429, 500, 502, 503, 504
Does NOT retry on 400, 401, 403, 404, 405, 422 (client errors)
Uses exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s (3 attempts total by default)
Logs retry attempts when verbose mode is enabled (uses logger)
After all retries exhausted, throws with the last error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build HTTP client with Basic Auth
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an HTTP client that authenticates with WordPress application passwords so that all API calls are authorized.
acceptanceCriteria: HTTP client in `src/api/client.ts` uses Bun's native `fetch` API
Adds `Authorization: Basic base64(username:application_password)` header to every request
Supports GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE methods
Sets `Content-Type: application/json` for POST/PUT/PATCH requests
Respects `timeout` from resolved config using `AbortSignal.timeout()`
Respects `verify_ssl` setting
Integrates with the retry helper from `src/helpers/retry.ts` for transient failures
Integrates with the error helper from `src/helpers/error.ts` for error formatting
Returns typed `ApiResponse` with `status`, `data`, `headers`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Discover API schema from /wp-json
userStories.description: As a user, I want the CLI to fetch my site's API schema so that it knows what routes and parameters are available.
acceptanceCriteria: Discovery module in `src/api/discovery.ts` sends GET request to `{host}{api_prefix}` (e.g., `https://example.com/wp-json`)
Parses the `routes` object from the WordPress REST API index response
Extracts for each route: path pattern, HTTP methods, accepted parameters (name, type, required, description, enum values)
Handles namespaced routes (e.g., `/wp/v2/posts`, `/my-plugin/v1/widgets`)
Returns a `DiscoveredSchema` object containing all parsed route metadata
Throws clear error (exit code 3) on 401/403 via the error helper
Throws clear error (exit code 6) on network/timeout failure via the error helper
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Map REST routes to CLI commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I need discovered routes transformed into CLI command metadata so that the parser can match user input to API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema mapper in `src/api/schema.ts` maps route paths to resource names: `/wp/v2/posts` -> `post`, `/wp/v2/categories` -> `category`
Singularizes resource names (strip trailing `s`, handle `ies` -> `y`, `sses` -> `ss`)
Maps HTTP methods to actions: GET (with ID param) -> `get`, GET (without) -> `list`, POST -> `create`, PUT/PATCH -> `update`, DELETE -> `delete`
Preserves parameter metadata (name, type, required, description, enum) for each action
Handles routes with path parameters like `/wp/v2/posts/(?P<id>[\d]+)` — extracts `id` as a required param for get/update/delete
Handles plugin namespaces: `/my-plugin/v1/widgets` -> resource `widget`
Returns a map of `{ [resource]: { [action]: CommandMeta } }`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cache discovered schema to disk and memory
userStories.description: As a user, I want schema discovery to be fast on repeated runs so that the CLI feels instant after the first use.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful discovery, writes schema to `~/.config/wpklx/cache/{host-hash}.json`
On subsequent runs, loads from disk cache first, then holds in memory for the session
Cache file includes a `discoveredAt` timestamp
Default TTL is 1 hour — if cache is older, re-discovers automatically
TTL is configurable via `WP_CACHE_TTL` env var or `cache_ttl` profile field (in seconds)
Provides a `forceRefresh` option that bypasses cache entirely (used by `wpklx discover`)
Creates cache directory if it doesn't exist
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build ANSI terminal renderer using Bun markdown
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a markdown-to-ANSI renderer so that help text and structured output looks good in the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Uses `Bun.markdown.render()` with custom ANSI escape code callbacks
Renders headings as bold + underlined text
Renders `**bold**` as ANSI bold, `*italic*` as ANSI italic
Renders inline `code` with a dimmed/highlighted style
Renders code blocks with language label
Renders tables with box-drawing characters and column alignment
Renders lists (ordered and unordered) with proper indentation
Renders horizontal rules as terminal-width lines
All ANSI output is disabled when `--no-color` flag is set or `NO_COLOR` env var is present
Exported as a reusable function from `src/cli/output.ts`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement table output formatter
userStories.description: As a user, I want `list` results displayed as a formatted table so that I can scan data quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Formats array responses as an aligned table with column headers
Auto-detects columns from the first response object's keys
`--fields id,title,status` limits which columns are shown
Truncates long values to fit terminal width
Uses the ANSI renderer for table styling (box-drawing characters, header highlighting)
Falls back to simple pipe-delimited format when `--no-color` is set
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement JSON and YAML output formatters
userStories.description: As a user, I want to output results as JSON or YAML so that I can pipe them to other tools.
acceptanceCriteria: `--format json` outputs pretty-printed JSON to stdout with 2-space indentation
`--format yaml` outputs YAML to stdout
`--fields` filters which keys are included in JSON/YAML output
`--quiet` mode outputs only the `id` field(s), one per line
Output goes to stdout (not stderr) so it can be piped
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement verbose and quiet output modes
userStories.description: As a user, I want verbose mode for debugging and quiet mode for scripting.
acceptanceCriteria: `--verbose` logs to stderr: request method, URL, headers (masked auth), response status, timing, response headers
`--verbose` shows retry attempts with delay info
`--quiet` suppresses all output except the resource ID(s), one per line
`--quiet` on `create` outputs just the new resource's ID
`--quiet` on `list` outputs one ID per line
`--quiet` on `delete` outputs nothing (just exit code)
Verbose and quiet are mutually exclusive — if both are passed, verbose wins with a warning
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement `wpklx version` command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to check the installed CLI version.
acceptanceCriteria: `wpklx version` or `wpklx --version` or `wpklx -v` prints `wpklx v{version}` reading version from `package.json`
Exits with code 0
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement `wpklx discover` command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to explicitly trigger API discovery so that I can refresh the cached schema when my site's API changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetches schema from the configured WordPress site using the discovery module
Always bypasses cache (force fresh discovery)
`wpklx discover --force` is an alias for the same behavior
Saves result to disk cache via the cache module
Prints summary: number of routes discovered, namespaces found, resource names
Exits with code 0 on success
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement `wpklx routes` command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all discovered API routes so that I know what commands are available.
acceptanceCriteria: Loads schema from cache (or triggers discovery if no cache exists)
Displays routes as a formatted table with columns: Resource, Action, HTTP Method, Path
Groups routes by resource
Supports `--format json` to output as JSON instead of table
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement `wpklx config` read commands (ls, show, path)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view my profile configuration from the CLI so that I can verify settings.
acceptanceCriteria: `wpklx config ls` lists all profile names, marks the default with `*`, marks the active one if specified via `@name`
`wpklx config show` shows the active profile's settings with password masked as `****`
`wpklx config show @name` shows a specific profile's settings with password masked
`wpklx config path` prints the path to the config file currently in use
Outputs through the table/text formatter
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement `wpklx config` write commands (add, rm, default)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manage my profiles from the CLI so that I don't have to manually edit YAML files.
acceptanceCriteria: `wpklx config add <name>` interactively prompts for host, username, application_password, and optional settings; writes to YAML file
`wpklx config rm <name>` removes a profile from the YAML file; refuses to remove the default profile unless another is set as default
`wpklx config default <name>` sets the default profile in the YAML file
Creates `~/.config/wpklx/config.yaml` if no config file exists when running `config add`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement `wpklx help` and resource-specific help
userStories.description: As a user, I want contextual help so that I know how to use any command.
acceptanceCriteria: `wpklx help` or `wpklx --help` shows global help: syntax, available built-in commands, global flags, example usage
`wpklx <resource> help` or `wpklx <resource> --help` shows help for a specific resource: available actions, accepted parameters for each action (name, type, required, description)
Help text is rendered using `Bun.markdown.render()` with ANSI callbacks from the output module for terminal formatting
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Execute dynamic resource commands (CRUD)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run `wpklx post list`, `wpklx post get --id 42`, etc. and have them call the correct WordPress API endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: `list` / `ls` sends GET to the collection endpoint (e.g., `/wp/v2/posts`), passes query params from CLI options
`get` / `show` sends GET to the item endpoint (e.g., `/wp/v2/posts/42`), requires `--id` or positional ID
`create` / `new` sends POST to the collection endpoint with CLI options as JSON body
`update` / `edit` sends PUT/PATCH to the item endpoint with CLI options as JSON body, requires `--id` or positional ID
`delete` / `rm` sends DELETE to the item endpoint, requires `--id` or positional ID, supports `--force` for permanent deletion
Passes `--per-page` and `--page` as query params for list actions
Sends request through the HTTP client (with auth, retry, timeout)
Pipes the API response to the appropriate output formatter (table/json/yaml based on --format)
Suggests similar resource names if the requested resource doesn't exist (Levenshtein distance fuzzy match)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement media upload command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to upload files to WordPress media library from the CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: `wpklx media upload --file ./photo.jpg` uploads the file to `/wp/v2/media`
Reads file from disk using Bun's `Bun.file()` API
Sends as `multipart/form-data` with correct `Content-Disposition` header
Supports `--title` to set the media title
Supports `--alt-text` to set alt text
Returns the created media object through the output formatter
Typecheck passes
